pattern: A board is reachable exactly when the counts of the two marks differ by the right amount and at most one player shows a completed line consistent with whose turn it was.
hints: Count the two marks. Since the first player moves first and they alternate, the first mark's count must equal or exceed the second's by at most one.
If a player has three in a line they must have made the final move, which pins down whose count should be larger.
Reject boards where both players have a winning line, where the first player wins but the counts are equal, or where the second player wins but the first has more marks; otherwise accept.

edge_inputs.description: first player took two turns in a row
empty board is reachable (start of game)
first player has a row win but counts are equal
both players show a completed line
first player wins on a diagonal with one more mark
second player wins but first still has more marks (play continued)

stress.description: random boards of all three characters, exercising every count/win combination
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cells = [rng.choice(' XO') for _ in range(9)]
    board = [''.join(cells[r*3:r*3+3]) for r in range(3)]
    return [board]
stress.note: random fills hit valid and many distinct invalid configurations
